vocabulary.example_cn: 他被安葬在家族墓地。

vocabulary.en: to bury a dead person
vocabulary.excerpt: That is where he will be laid to rest.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他被安葬在故乡。

vocabulary.en: to make something seem less important or less noticeable
vocabulary.excerpt: Iran's Revolutionary Guard accused the U.S. of trying to overshadow the final burial with the strike.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她的成功使我的成就黯然失色。

vocabulary.en: to mention someone or something in an indirect way
vocabulary.excerpt: the president even sort of alluded to, well, they're in the middle of funeral proceedings.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他暗示可能辞职。

vocabulary.en: complete failure of diplomatic relations or negotiations
vocabulary.excerpt: it shows there's diplomatic collapse right now.
vocabulary.example_cn: 两国关系陷入外交崩溃。

vocabulary.en: in a way that shows doubt or disbelief
vocabulary.excerpt: looked at that MOU skeptically
vocabulary.example_cn: 她怀疑地看着他。

vocabulary.en: very easy to predict
vocabulary.excerpt: this was highly predictable.
vocabulary.example_cn: 结果高度可预测。

vocabulary.en: a person who tries to help two sides reach an agreement
vocabulary.excerpt: until hopefully we see some intermediary step in
vocabulary.example_cn: 他充当了调解人。

vocabulary.en: in the most important or leading position
vocabulary.excerpt: At the forefront, of course, is the office of Iran's supreme leader.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这家公司处于技术前沿。

vocabulary.en: the place or group where most power is held
vocabulary.excerpt: the power center has sort of shifted a little bit.
vocabulary.example_cn: 权力中心已转移。

vocabulary.en: to state clearly and firmly that something must be done
vocabulary.excerpt: what exactly specifically this Article Five of the memorandum of understanding stipulates
vocabulary.example_cn: 合同规定付款期限。

vocabulary.en: the right to pass through a place without restrictions
vocabulary.excerpt: allow free transit
vocabulary.example_cn: 船只享有自由通行权。

vocabulary.en: to be placed in a public place so that people can see the body before a funeral
vocabulary.excerpt: the bodies lay in state
vocabulary.example_cn: 前总统的遗体供人瞻仰。

vocabulary.en: very long and tiring
vocabulary.excerpt: this is really a marathon funeral proceeding
vocabulary.example_cn: 马拉松式的会议持续了12小时。

vocabulary.en: a temporary relief from something unpleasant
vocabulary.excerpt: after this brief reprieve
vocabulary.example_cn: 雨停给了我们短暂的缓解。

vocabulary.en: in a way that uses a mild or indirect word or phrase
vocabulary.excerpt: what people have been euphemistically calling kinetic action
vocabulary.example_cn: 他委婉地称其为‘重组’。

vocabulary.en: military action, used as a euphemism
vocabulary.excerpt: what people have been euphemistically calling kinetic action
vocabulary.example_cn: 政府避免使用‘战争’一词，而用‘军事行动’。

vocabulary.en: stockpiles of oil held by a country to ensure energy security
vocabulary.excerpt: we've got strategic oil reserves around the world that are at all-time lows
vocabulary.example_cn: 各国维持战略石油储备以应对危机。

vocabulary.en: to make something less severe
vocabulary.excerpt: this was supposed to alleviate
vocabulary.example_cn: 药物缓解了他的疼痛。

vocabulary.word: up like a rocket down like a feather
vocabulary.phonetic: /ʌp laɪk ə ˈrɑːkɪt daʊn laɪk ə ˈfeðər/
vocabulary.cn: 涨如火箭跌如羽毛（指价格涨快跌慢）
vocabulary.en: prices rise quickly but fall slowly
vocabulary.excerpt: You heard the up like a rocket down like a feather analogy over and over again.
vocabulary.example_cn: 汽油价格总是涨如火箭跌如羽毛。

vocabulary.en: a ship used to remove mines from the sea
vocabulary.excerpt: we could actually get a minesweeper in there
vocabulary.example_cn: 海军派出扫雷舰清理航道。

vocabulary.en: a person who signs a document together with another person
vocabulary.excerpt: The United States is a cosigner to that.
vocabulary.example_cn: 美国是该条约的共同签署国。

vocabulary.en: very critical and harsh
vocabulary.excerpt: delivering a pretty scathing critique of Israel
vocabulary.example_cn: 他对政府提出了严厉批评。

vocabulary.en: to cause a feeling or situation to exist
vocabulary.excerpt: America's silence through the years has engendered the worst of your domestic politics.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他的言论引发了争议。

vocabulary.en: to look away from something, especially to avoid seeing something unpleasant
vocabulary.excerpt: We've done you no favors by averting our eyes from, in my view, your misjudgments.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他回避了问题。

vocabulary.en: a wrong opinion or decision
vocabulary.excerpt: your misjudgments
vocabulary.example_cn: 这是一个严重的判断错误。

vocabulary.en: to impose a penalty on someone for breaking a rule
vocabulary.excerpt: every Israeli found attacking Palestinian civilians or their property in the future will be sanctioned.
vocabulary.example_cn: 违规者将受到制裁。

vocabulary.en: the process of learning something by repeating it many times without thinking about it
vocabulary.excerpt: It's sort of now almost like rote
vocabulary.example_cn: 他靠死记硬背学习。

vocabulary.cn: 自我憎恨的犹太人（贬义）
vocabulary.en: a Jewish person who is perceived to be critical of Israel or Jewish interests
vocabulary.excerpt: they weren't called self-hating Jews by Benjamin Netanyahu
vocabulary.example_cn: 他被指责为自我憎恨的犹太人。

vocabulary.en: a test that uses a single factor to decide whether something is acceptable or not
vocabulary.excerpt: people want this to be a litmus test in 2028
vocabulary.example_cn: 这个问题成为候选人的试金石。

vocabulary.en: something used to hide an embarrassing or unpleasant situation
vocabulary.excerpt: This is Rahm Emanuel putting on a fig leaf
vocabulary.example_cn: 他的道歉只是遮羞布。

vocabulary.en: to successfully deal with a difficult situation by finding a middle ground
vocabulary.excerpt: How are you going to thread that needle?
vocabulary.example_cn: 他必须在两派之间巧妙周旋。

vocabulary.en: a division into two completely opposite groups
vocabulary.excerpt: there is a huge dichotomy between those
vocabulary.example_cn: 理想与现实之间存在巨大分歧。

vocabulary.en: a person who claims to represent the interests of ordinary people
vocabulary.excerpt: a dark horse, a populist
vocabulary.example_cn: 这位民粹主义政治家赢得了支持。

vocabulary.cn: α-半乳糖综合征（一种由蜱虫叮咬引起的红肉过敏症）
vocabulary.en: a condition caused by a tick bite that makes a person allergic to red meat and dairy
vocabulary.excerpt: It's alpha gal syndrome.
vocabulary.example_cn: α-半乳糖综合征在美国越来越常见。

sentences.en: A U.S. official telling CNN the ceasefire with Iran has, quote, temporarily ceased, but the situation remains dynamic.
sentences.cn: 一位美国官员告诉CNN，与伊朗的停火协议“暂时停止”，但局势仍然动态变化。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句，插入语quote
sentences.analysis: 主句为A U.S. official telling CNN，后接宾语从句the ceasefire...ceased，quote表示引用，but连接并列句。

sentences.en: Iran also claiming that it targeted Qatar in the retaliation against these U.S. military assets.
sentences.cn: 伊朗还声称，在针对这些美国军事资产的报复行动中，它袭击了卡塔尔。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句，介词短语作状语
sentences.analysis: 主句Iran also claiming，后接that引导的宾语从句，in the retaliation...作状语修饰targeted。

sentences.en: The Secretary General reiterates that a return to full-scale hostilities would have catastrophic consequences for the peoples of the region, for international peace and security, and for the global economy.
sentences.cn: 秘书长重申，恢复全面敌对行动将对该地区人民、国际和平与安全以及全球经济造成灾难性后果。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句，并列介词短语
sentences.analysis: 主句The Secretary General reiterates，后接that宾语从句，从句中for...并列三个对象。

sentences.en: We are seeing the number of vessels managing to get through that critical waterway lesson because of the increase in hostilities.
sentences.cn: 我们看到，由于敌对行动增加，能够通过这条关键水道的船只数量减少了。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句，原因状语
sentences.analysis: 主句We are seeing，后接省略that的宾语从句，managing to get through作定语修饰vessels，because of...作原因状语。

sentences.en: They believe that they are in control of the Strait of Hormuz, that this memorandum gives them control over the logistics, the coordination, and all ships transiting through have to coordinate with them and any that are trying to avoid that and go via the Omani route, some of them are being targeted.
sentences.cn: 他们认为自己控制着霍尔木兹海峡，这份备忘录赋予他们对物流和协调的控制权，所有过境船只都必须与他们协调，任何试图避开并经由阿曼航线的船只，其中一些正成为目标。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+并列宾语从句，并列谓语
sentences.analysis: 主句They believe，后接两个that宾语从句，第二个从句中and连接并列谓语have to coordinate和are being targeted，any that...作主语。

sentences.en: One of the things, though, that the leadership here says is they say that he's fully in charge, he's fully in control.
sentences.cn: 不过，这里的领导层所说的一件事是，他们说他完全负责，完全掌控。
sentences.structure: 主系表+定语从句+表语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句One of the things is，that the leadership here says是定语从句修饰things，表语从句they say that...，其中that引导宾语从句。

sentences.en: And one of the things, by the way, that we also have to keep in mind is that the office of Supreme Leader is actually a pretty large body.
sentences.cn: 顺便说一句，我们还必须记住的一件事是，最高领袖办公室实际上是一个相当大的机构。
sentences.structure: 主系表+定语从句+表语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句one of the things is，that we also have to keep in mind是定语从句，表语从句that the office...。

sentences.en: You'll recall that Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the early hours of the U.S. and Israel's attacks on Iran in a massive airstrike here in Tehran on his headquarters in the center of the city on February 28.
sentences.cn: 你会记得，阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊是在2月28日凌晨，美国和以色列对伊朗发动袭击时，在德黑兰市中心他的总部遭到大规模空袭而丧生的。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句，时间地点状语
sentences.analysis: 主句You'll recall，后接that宾语从句，从句中in the early hours...和in a massive airstrike...等作状语。

sentences.en: And if you look at what's been going on here over the past couple of days, it's been one event after another.
sentences.cn: 如果你看看过去几天这里发生的事情，那是一个接一个的事件。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句+主句
sentences.analysis: if引导条件状语从句，从句中what's been going on是宾语从句，主句it's been one event after another。

sentences.en: So, as you can imagine, this is a huge logistical operation on the part of the authorities, on the part of organizations that are pitching in as well.
sentences.cn: 所以，可以想象，这对当局和参与其中的组织来说都是一项巨大的后勤行动。
sentences.structure: 插入语+主系表+并列介词短语+定语从句
sentences.analysis: as you can imagine是插入语，主句this is a huge logistical operation，on the part of...并列，that are pitching in定语从句修饰organizations。

sentences.en: And I think that it -- the power, the people who are in power here in this country certainly see that as also shoring up the support that they have as they are in these negotiations with the United States and now, again, in that standoff with the United States as well.
sentences.cn: 我认为，这个国家的掌权者当然也将其视为巩固他们拥有的支持，因为他们正在与美国进行谈判，现在又再次与美国陷入僵持。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句，插入语+定语从句+状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句I think，后接that宾语从句，从句中the power是插入语，the people who...是主语，see that as...，that they have定语从句修饰support，as引导原因状语从句。

sentences.en: And what I'm hearing between all of this is really the lack of three things that caused him to fly home on the older Air Force One.
sentences.cn: 我从这一切中听到的实际上是缺乏三样东西，导致他乘坐较旧的空军一号飞回家。
sentences.structure: 主语从句+系表+定语从句
sentences.analysis: what I'm hearing是主语从句，主句is the lack of three things，that caused...定语从句修饰things。

sentences.en: Additionally, it probably doesn't have the full secure communications network that President Trump would also need to essentially prosecute a war from afar with some of the strikes on Iran that we've seen in the past two days.
sentences.cn: 此外，它可能没有完整的保密通信网络，而特朗普总统也需要这些网络来从远处进行战争，包括我们过去两天看到的对伊朗的一些打击。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+定语从句+不定式目的状语
sentences.analysis: 主句it doesn't have the network，that President Trump would need定语从句，to prosecute...不定式作目的状语，that we've seen定语从句修饰strikes。

sentences.en: Some of those Air Force One airplanes really were built to be survivable under even the worst things like being able to survive a potential nuclear blast.
sentences.cn: 其中一些空军一号飞机确实被建造得即使在最坏的情况下也能生存，比如能够承受潜在的核爆炸。
sentences.structure: 主谓+不定式目的状语+介词短语
sentences.analysis: 主句Some of those airplanes were built，to be survivable不定式作目的状语，like being able to...介词短语举例。

sentences.en: And I think that's exactly why we're seeing him fly home on some of the older jets.
sentences.cn: 我认为这正是我们看到他在一些较旧的飞机上飞回家的原因。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+表语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句I think，后接that宾语从句（省略that），从句中that's exactly why...是表语从句，why引导原因状语从句。

sentences.en: And what they've done is tried to make it work.
sentences.cn: 他们所做的就是试图让它工作。
sentences.structure: 主语从句+系表+不定式短语
sentences.analysis: what they've done是主语从句，主句is tried to make it work，tried是过去分词作表语？实际应为is to try，但口语中省略to。

sentences.en: Iran lacks capabilities with the correct ranges to be able to come after some of, you know, some of where the president would be.
sentences.cn: 伊朗缺乏适当射程的能力来攻击总统可能所在的一些地方。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+不定式目的状语+插入语
sentences.analysis: 主句Iran lacks capabilities，with the correct ranges作定语，to be able to come after不定式目的状语，you know插入语，where the president would be定语从句修饰some。

sentences.en: And you've got some people within the Iranian system who are saying, you know what? We want to change our relationship with the United States.
sentences.cn: 伊朗体制内有一些人在说，你知道吗？我们想改变与美国的关系。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+定语从句+直接引语
sentences.analysis: 主句You've got some people，who are saying定语从句，直接引语We want to change...。

sentences.en: The United States military does retain significant capabilities within the Middle East.
sentences.cn: 美国军方在中东确实保留了重要的军事能力。
sentences.structure: 主语 + 谓语（does retain）+ 宾语 + 状语
sentences.analysis: 使用助动词does强调谓语retain，表示‘确实保留’。

sentences.en: Can Iran be bombed into submission over time?
sentences.cn: 伊朗能通过持续轰炸被炸得屈服吗？
sentences.structure: 情态动词 + 主语 + 被动语态 + 状语
sentences.analysis: 被动语态be bombed into submission，into表示结果状态。

sentences.en: This war started February 28. President Trump said then that this was about to be, you know, around four to six weeks.
sentences.cn: 这场战争始于2月28日。特朗普总统当时说，这大概会持续四到六周。
sentences.structure: 简单句 + 复合句（主句+宾语从句）
sentences.analysis: 宾语从句中be about to表示‘即将’，you know为插入语。

sentences.en: Now I think we're looking at a protracted conflict, and the negotiations are going to be in trouble for a while.
sentences.cn: 现在我认为我们面临一场持久冲突，谈判将暂时陷入困境。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 宾语从句 + 并列句
sentences.analysis: look at在此意为‘面临’，be going to表示将来。

sentences.en: This is the problem with the MOU that was signed between the United States and Iran.
sentences.cn: 这就是美伊签署的谅解备忘录的问题所在。
sentences.structure: 主系表 + 定语从句
sentences.analysis: that引导定语从句修饰MOU。

sentences.en: And because the details — I mean, there was 14 points, 1 1/2 pages. I mean, I think we've talked about having grocery lists that are longer than the MOU.
sentences.cn: 因为细节——我是说，只有14点，1.5页。我是说，我想我们讨论过比备忘录还长的购物清单。
sentences.structure: 原因状语从句 + 插入语 + 主句 + 宾语从句 + 定语从句
sentences.analysis: I mean为插入语，that引导定语从句修饰grocery lists。

sentences.en: But this is really the problem. Both sides signed a very murky agreement and now to implement it and execute it it's proving to be very difficult as we're seeing kinetic action over the last few days.
sentences.cn: 但这确实是问题所在。双方签署了一份非常模糊的协议，现在要实施和执行它，事实证明非常困难，正如我们过去几天看到的军事行动。
sentences.structure: 简单句 + 并列句 + 不定式短语 + 主句 + 状语从句
sentences.analysis: to implement it and execute it为不定式短语作主语，it为形式主语；as引导时间状语从句。

sentences.en: I think when it gets beyond those sites along the strait and we see strikes within central Iran — the western part of the country — that's when we're going to see a larger expansion of this war.
sentences.cn: 我认为，当打击超出海峡沿岸地点，我们看到伊朗中部——该国西部——遭到打击时，那才是我们将看到战争更大规模扩大之时。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 宾语从句（内含时间状语从句+主句）
sentences.analysis: when引导时间状语从句，主句为that's when...，when引导表语从句。

sentences.en: It is just depleting of not only just munitions but time and energy.
sentences.cn: 这不仅消耗弹药，还消耗时间和精力。
sentences.structure: 主系表 + 介词短语
sentences.analysis: depleting of...为形容词短语，not only...but...连接并列成分。

sentences.en: And they were really the ones that said to this administration we need to stop this war and put some type of ceasefire into place because we just can't handle the level of strikes that are coming towards our country right now.
sentences.cn: 他们正是那些对政府说我们需要停止这场战争并实施某种停火的人，因为我们无法承受目前针对我国的打击力度。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 定语从句 + 宾语从句 + 原因状语从句 + 定语从句
sentences.analysis: 第一个that引导定语从句修饰ones；第二个that引导定语从句修饰strikes。

sentences.en: Now this all comes after allegations of sexual misconduct from several women, which caused a mass exodus of endorsements and contributions to his campaign.
sentences.cn: 这一切发生在多名女性指控其性行为不端之后，这些指控导致其竞选活动的支持和捐款大量流失。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 时间状语从句 + 非限制性定语从句
sentences.analysis: which引导非限制性定语从句，指代allegations。

sentences.en: President Trump has been trying to delay paying Carroll after he was found liable for sexually abusing and defaming her.
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统一直试图拖延向卡罗尔支付赔偿，此前他被认定对性虐待和诽谤她负有责任。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 时间状语从句
sentences.analysis: has been trying为现在完成进行时；after引导时间状语从句。

sentences.en: He announced the U.S. will be buying Ukrainian drones and more significantly will be teaming with Ukraine to co-produce Patriot missile interceptors — a development that could significantly improve Kyiv's air defenses.
sentences.cn: 他宣布美国将购买乌克兰无人机，更重要的是，将与乌克兰合作生产爱国者导弹拦截器——这一进展可能显著改善基辅的防空能力。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 宾语从句 + 同位语 + 定语从句
sentences.analysis: a development为同位语，that引导定语从句修饰development。

sentences.en: This way he can't complain that we're not giving them enough.
sentences.cn: 这样他就不能抱怨我们给得不够了。
sentences.structure: 状语 + 主句 + 宾语从句
sentences.analysis: This way作状语，that引导宾语从句。

sentences.en: And I think for President Trump to see Ukraine not only being able to strike inside Moscow but then, of course, to defend its territory — if he can add this extra nudge maybe he thinks that can get Putin to a deal.
sentences.cn: 我认为，对于特朗普总统来说，看到乌克兰不仅能够打击莫斯科境内，而且还能保卫其领土——如果他能再推一把，也许他认为这能让普京达成协议。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 宾语从句（内含不定式短语+条件状语从句+宾语从句）
sentences.analysis: for President Trump to see...为不定式复合结构作主语；if引导条件状语从句；that引导宾语从句。

sentences.en: So when the mood music changes, as it did with President Trump yesterday where the United States and NATO and our European allies are essentially talking from the same sheet — that is a sign to Putin that he can't break the alliance.
sentences.cn: 所以，当氛围发生变化——就像昨天特朗普总统那样，美国、北约和我们的欧洲盟友基本上步调一致——这对普京来说是一个信号，表明他无法破坏联盟。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句 + 方式状语从句 + 定语从句 + 主句 + 同位语从句
sentences.analysis: as引导方式状语从句；where引导定语从句修饰yesterday；that引导同位语从句修饰sign。

sentences.en: Those who provided their territories, military bases, and infrastructure to enable the aggression cannot evade responsibility for their contribution to an unprovoked aggression and its grave consequences.
sentences.cn: 那些提供领土、军事基地和基础设施以促成侵略行为的人，无法逃避他们对无端侵略及其严重后果所应承担的责任。
sentences.structure: 主句：Those...cannot evade responsibility；定语从句：who provided...；介词短语：for their contribution...
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句主语为Those，后接who引导的定语从句修饰Those，谓语为cannot evade，宾语为responsibility，后接for引导的介词短语说明责任的内容。

sentences.en: And to me, that's a signal that the U.S. is trying to send to Iran to tell them that, if these strikes continue, and if things escalate further, the U.S. is going to go after infrastructure that is not only military in nature, but also civilian in use.
sentences.cn: 对我来说，这是一个信号，表明美国试图向伊朗传达：如果这些打击继续下去，如果局势进一步升级，美国将打击那些不仅具有军事性质，而且用于民用目的的基础设施。
sentences.structure: 主句：that's a signal；同位语从句：that the U.S. is trying to send...；不定式短语：to tell them that...；条件状语从句：if these strikes continue...；定语从句：that is not only...
sentences.analysis: 这是一个多重复合句，主句为that's a signal，后接同位语从句解释signal的内容，同位语从句中包含不定式短语作目的状语，不定式短语中又包含that引导的宾语从句，宾语从句中包含两个if引导的条件状语从句和一个定语从句修饰infrastructure。

sentences.en: And I think that's very much what we're seeing here.
sentences.cn: 我认为这正是我们在这里所看到的。
sentences.structure: 主句：I think；宾语从句：that's very much what we're seeing here；表语从句：what we're seeing here
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为I think，后接that引导的宾语从句，宾语从句中又包含what引导的表语从句。

sentences.en: And for now, I don't think either side wants to return to high-intensity conflict, which is why you're going to continue to see these kinds of tit-for-tat strikes, these -- this kind of skirmishing, which, frankly, has become a feature of the conflict rather than a bug.
sentences.cn: 就目前而言，我认为双方都不希望回到高强度冲突，这就是为什么你会继续看到这种针锋相对的打击，这种小规模冲突，坦率地说，已经成为冲突的一个特征，而不是一个缺陷。
sentences.structure: 主句：I don't think；宾语从句：either side wants to return...；非限制性定语从句：which is why...；非限制性定语从句：which...has become...
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为I don't think，后接宾语从句，宾语从句后接which引导的非限制性定语从句，修饰整个宾语从句，该定语从句中又包含why引导的表语从句，表语从句中又包含which引导的非限制性定语从句修饰skirmishing。

sentences.en: But that doesn't mean that there aren't risks for escalation.
sentences.cn: 但这并不意味着没有升级的风险。
sentences.structure: 主句：that doesn't mean；宾语从句：that there aren't risks...
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句主语为that，谓语为doesn't mean，后接that引导的宾语从句，宾语从句为there be句型的否定形式。

sentences.en: And I also feel an immense amount of responsibility to everyone who has worked so hard to get us to where we are.
sentences.cn: 我也对每一个努力工作让我们走到今天的人感到巨大的责任。
sentences.structure: 主句：I feel responsibility；定语从句：who has worked...；宾语从句：where we are
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为I feel an immense amount of responsibility，后接to everyone，everyone后接who引导的定语从句，定语从句中包含where引导的宾语从句作to的宾语。

sentences.en: And the fact that the Democratic Party stood with him after the first allegations, and the men of the Democratic Party stood with him is disgusting.
sentences.cn: 民主党在第一次指控后仍然支持他，而且民主党的男性支持他，这一事实令人作呕。
sentences.structure: 主语：the fact；同位语从句：that the Democratic Party stood...；并列谓语：and the men...stood；系表结构：is disgusting
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主语为the fact，后接that引导的同位语从句说明fact的内容，同位语从句中包含两个并列的谓语stood，主句谓语为is，表语为disgusting。

sentences.en: And there's this question of will the remaining candidates be tainted by association with him?
sentences.cn: 还有一个问题：剩下的候选人是否会因为与他有关联而受到玷污？
sentences.structure: 主句：there's this question；介词短语：of will...；宾语从句：will the remaining candidates be tainted...
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为there be句型，后接of介词短语，of后接一个疑问句形式的宾语从句。

sentences.en: And I feel like he owes us all an apology for forcing a candidate like that into the system, who obviously backfired.
sentences.cn: 我觉得他欠我们所有人一个道歉，因为他把这样一个候选人强行纳入体系，而这个人显然适得其反。
sentences.structure: 主句：I feel；宾语从句：he owes us an apology；介词短语：for forcing...；定语从句：who obviously backfired
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为I feel，后接like引导的宾语从句（口语化用法），宾语从句中谓语为owes，后接for引导的介词短语说明原因，介词短语中forcing的宾语candidate后接who引导的定语从句。

sentences.en: And I think that's very much what we're seeing here.
sentences.cn: 我认为这正是我们在这里所看到的。
sentences.structure: 主句：I think；宾语从句：that's very much what we're seeing here；表语从句：what we're seeing here
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为I think，后接that引导的宾语从句，宾语从句中又包含what引导的表语从句。

sentences.en: Mourners traveling from Iran's capital may have been delayed after a reported U.S. strike on railways connecting the two cities.
sentences.cn: 据报道，美国对连接两座城市的铁路发动了袭击，从伊朗首都出发的送葬者可能因此延误。
sentences.structure: 主句（Mourners...may have been delayed）+ 时间状语（after...strike on railways...）
sentences.analysis: 主句使用情态动词may have been delayed表示对过去可能性的推测；after引导时间状语从句，其中reported作定语修饰strike，connecting the two cities为现在分词短语作定语修饰railways。

sentences.en: The MOU was so broad and there were so many holes within it that both sides misinterpreted each other.
sentences.cn: 谅解备忘录过于宽泛，漏洞百出，导致双方误解了对方。
sentences.structure: so...that...结构（so broad...so many holes...that...）
sentences.analysis: so...that...引导结果状语从句，表示‘如此…以至于…’。两个so分别修饰broad和many，强调程度。

sentences.en: And now with some of the friction that you're seeing, especially in the Strait of Hormuz, it does certainly seem as though there is definitely a different understanding on the U.S. side and on the Iranian side as to what exactly specifically this Article Five of the memorandum of understanding stipulates, which essentially calls on Iran to set the conditions for a free transition of the Strait of Hormuz, but the Iranians clearly interpret that as them having the say of how the Strait can be transited, whereas the U.S. believes it just means that the Iranians take obstacles out of the way and allow free transit, even if that is via the route that goes through Omani territorial waters.
sentences.cn: 现在，随着你看到的摩擦，特别是在霍尔木兹海峡，美国方面和伊朗方面对于谅解备忘录第五条的具体规定显然存在不同理解。该条款基本上要求伊朗为霍尔木兹海峡的自由通行创造条件，但伊朗人明确将其解释为他们有权决定如何通行海峡，而美国则认为这只是意味着伊朗清除障碍并允许自由通行，即使是通过阿曼领海的路线。
sentences.structure: 主句（it does certainly seem as though...）+ 同位语从句（as to what...stipulates）+ 定语从句（which essentially calls on...）+ 并列从句（but the Iranians interpret...whereas the U.S. believes...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复杂长句。主句使用it seems as though引导表语从句；as to what...stipulates是介词短语作状语，其中what引导宾语从句；which引导非限制性定语从句修饰Article Five；but和whereas连接两个并列从句，对比伊朗和美国的解释；even if引导让步状语从句，其中that goes through...是定语从句修饰route。

sentences.en: The hard truth is that America's silence through the years has engendered the worst of your domestic politics.
sentences.cn: 严酷的事实是，美国多年来的沉默催生了你们国内政治中最糟糕的一面。
sentences.structure: 主句（The hard truth is）+ 表语从句（that America's silence...has engendered...）
sentences.analysis: that引导表语从句，从句主语为America's silence，谓语has engendered，宾语the worst of your domestic politics。

sentences.en: For too long, American policy towards Israel operated under the assumption that the best thing Washington can do for Jerusalem is to blindly, to silently stand behind your government, without conditions, without demands, without consequences, even when we disagreed.
sentences.cn: 长期以来，美国对以色列的政策基于这样一种假设：华盛顿能为耶路撒冷做的最好的事情就是盲目地、默默地支持你们的政府，没有条件、没有要求、没有后果，即使我们不同意。
sentences.structure: 主句（American policy...operated under the assumption）+ 同位语从句（that the best thing...is to...）+ 让步状语从句（even when we disagreed）
sentences.analysis: that引导同位语从句解释assumption的内容；从句中主语为the best thing，谓语is，不定式to blindly, to silently stand behind...作表语；without conditions等为伴随状语；even when引导让步状语从句。

sentences.en: And I think that Democrats, they are -- we are in this weird where the establishment is very pro-Israel and then we have a lot of the very progressive base that's not.
sentences.cn: 我认为民主党人——我们处于一种奇怪的境地：建制派非常亲以色列，而我们有很多非常进步的基层选民却不亲以色列。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（that Democrats...are in this weird where...）
sentences.analysis: that引导宾语从句；从句中where引导定语从句修饰weird（此处weird作名词，意为‘奇怪境地’）；定语从句由and连接两个并列分句，第二个分句中that's not是定语从句修饰base。

sentences.en: And I think that's the line that's being drawn inside the party.
sentences.cn: 我认为这就是党内正在划定的界限。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（that's the line）+ 定语从句（that's being drawn inside the party）
sentences.analysis: 第一个that为指示代词作主语；第二个that引导定语从句修饰line，从句使用现在进行时的被动语态。

sentences.en: And that is when Democrats need to actually focus on what's going to get them elected.
sentences.cn: 那时民主党人需要真正关注什么能让他们当选。
sentences.structure: 主句（That is）+ 表语从句（when Democrats need to focus on...）+ 宾语从句（what's going to get them elected）
sentences.analysis: when引导表语从句；what引导宾语从句作focus on的宾语。

sentences.en: The support for Israel is dropping among the Democratic Party, but it doesn't even rank sometimes in the top five of issues that Democrats care about in swing states.
sentences.cn: 民主党内部对以色列的支持率正在下降，但这个问题有时甚至排不进摇摆州民主党人关心的前五大议题。
sentences.structure: 并列句（but连接两个分句）+ 定语从句（that Democrats care about in swing states）
sentences.analysis: 第一个分句为简单句；第二个分句中that引导定语从句修饰issues。

sentences.en: When Susan Collins voted to confirm Brett Kavanaugh, and he was the writer of Roe v. Wade being reversed, that's when women started turning against Susan Collins.
sentences.cn: 当苏珊·柯林斯投票确认布雷特·卡瓦诺时——而他是推翻罗诉韦德案的关键人物——女性开始反对苏珊·柯林斯。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句（When Susan Collins voted...）+ 插入语（and he was...）+ 主句（that's when...）
sentences.analysis: When引导时间状语从句；and he was...为并列句作插入语，补充说明；主句that's when...中when引导表语从句。

topics.title: 美伊冲突与停火破裂
topics.content: 2026年7月，美国与伊朗在经历短暂停火后再次爆发冲突。美国对伊朗90个军事目标实施打击，伊朗则报复性攻击美国在科威特和巴林的军事基地。局势紧张，联合国警告全面战争将带来灾难性后果。
topics.keywords: 美伊冲突, 停火, 军事打击, 霍尔木兹海峡, 联合国

topics.title: 伊朗最高领袖葬礼与权力交接
topics.content: 伊朗前最高领袖阿里·哈梅内伊在2月28日美以空袭中丧生，其葬礼持续一周，最终在伊朗马什哈德下葬。其子穆杰塔巴·哈梅内伊接任最高领袖，但未公开露面，仅通过书面声明沟通。葬礼期间民众反美情绪高涨。
topics.keywords: 伊朗最高领袖, 葬礼, 权力交接, 穆杰塔巴·哈梅内伊, 反美情绪

topics.title: 特朗普使用旧空军一号的安保争议
topics.content: 特朗普总统在结束土耳其访问后，未使用卡塔尔捐赠的新空军一号，而是改乘旧飞机。特勤局担忧新飞机的安保、通信和生存能力不足，尤其是在国际环境中。新飞机因匆忙改装而缺乏导弹防御和保密通信系统。
topics.keywords: 空军一号, 特朗普, 安保, 卡塔尔, 飞机改装

topics.title: 美伊冲突与霍尔木兹海峡
topics.content: 美国与伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡附近发生军事冲突。美国对伊朗沿海目标进行打击，伊朗则攻击美国军事基地和商船。双方签署的谅解备忘录（MOU）细节模糊，导致执行困难。伊朗试图控制海峡并收取通行费，美国则强调航行自由。冲突可能扩大，海湾国家担忧被卷入。
topics.keywords: Iran, Strait of Hormuz, MOU, freedom of navigation, military strikes

topics.title: 美国与乌克兰合作生产爱国者导弹拦截器
topics.content: 特朗普宣布美国将与乌克兰合作生产爱国者导弹拦截器，并购买乌克兰无人机。此举旨在提升乌克兰的防空能力，并被视为对乌克兰在无人机技术方面创新的认可。同时，乌克兰对莫斯科的打击改变了战场态势，特朗普对乌克兰的态度发生转变。
topics.keywords: Ukraine, Patriot missile, drone technology, air defense, NATO

topics.title: 美国政治与性侵指控
topics.content: 民主党参议员候选人Graham Platner因多名女性指控性行为不端而退出竞选，导致缅因州民主党需重新提名候选人。同时，特朗普被联邦法官命令向E. Jean Carroll支付近600万美元，因其被认定对性虐待和诽谤负有责任。
topics.keywords: Graham Platner, sexual misconduct, E. Jean Carroll, defamation, Maine Democratic Party

topics.title: 纽约曼哈顿高层建筑结构问题
topics.content: 纽约曼哈顿一栋高层建筑面临倒塌风险，施工人员从9层到屋顶每层加装钢支撑。纽约市建筑局正在调查结构失效的原因。
topics.keywords: Manhattan high-rise, structural failure, steel supports, New York Department of Buildings

topics.title: 美伊冲突与停火前景
topics.content: 美国与伊朗之间互相打击军事目标，美国打击了伊朗90多个目标，伊朗声称打击了美国在科威特和巴林的军事基地。伊朗外交部发言人警告海湾盟友和北约领导人，称提供领土和基础设施支持侵略者的人不能逃避责任。分析人士认为，双方都不希望回到全面战争，但低烈度冲突可能成为新常态。
topics.keywords: 美伊冲突, 停火, 军事打击, 霍尔木兹海峡, 低烈度冲突

topics.title: 格雷厄姆·普拉特纳退出缅因州参议员竞选
topics.content: 民主党候选人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳因性行为不端指控而暂停竞选活动，导致大量背书和捐款流失。他的竞选团队缺乏审查，被批评为“虚假候选人”。民主党内部对此反应不一，有人指责伯尼·桑德斯推动该候选人进入体系。
topics.keywords: 格雷厄姆·普拉特纳, 缅因州参议员, 性行为不端, 竞选审查, 民主党

topics.title: 美国政治中的“转移话题”现象
topics.content: 在讨论普拉特纳丑闻时，主持人指出存在“转移话题”现象，即用对方党派的问题来回避自身问题。例如，提及特朗普的性侵案来转移对普拉特纳的批评。
topics.keywords: 转移话题, 政治辩论, 党派之争, 双重标准

topics.title: 密歇根州寄生虫爆发
topics.content: 密歇根州爆发了该州历史上最大规模的寄生虫感染，近1000人确诊，导致数周的水样腹泻。其他28个州也有类似疫情报告，感染源难以确定，但通常通过受污染的食物传播。
topics.keywords: 寄生虫爆发, 密歇根州, 腹泻, 食品安全

topics.title: 派拉蒙收购华纳兄弟探索的反垄断诉讼
topics.content: 多个州的律师正在敲定一项反垄断诉讼，挑战派拉蒙对华纳兄弟探索的收购。诉讼称该交易将损害娱乐和新闻行业的竞争。派拉蒙回应称将继续与监管机构建设性接触。
topics.keywords: 反垄断, 派拉蒙, 华纳兄弟探索, 收购, 竞争

topics.title: 特朗普机场命名
topics.content: 佛罗里达州棕榈滩国际机场正式更名为“唐纳德·J·特朗普总统国际机场”，机场代码将改为“DJT”。有两起诉讼试图阻止这一更名。
topics.keywords: 特朗普, 机场命名, 棕榈滩, 诉讼

topics.title: 米奇·麦康奈尔的健康状况
topics.content: 84岁的参议员米奇·麦康奈尔已住院三周，原因不明。特朗普表示不知道他的情况。肯塔基州州长安迪·贝希尔要求麦康奈尔披露更多信息。
topics.keywords: 米奇·麦康奈尔, 住院, 健康状况, 特朗普

topics.title: 伊朗前最高领袖葬礼
topics.content: 伊朗为前最高领袖阿里·哈梅内伊举行马拉松式葬礼，遗体将被安葬在他的出生地东北部城市。
topics.keywords: 伊朗, 最高领袖, 葬礼, 哈梅内伊

topics.title: 伊朗最高领袖葬礼与美伊冲突
topics.content: 伊朗前最高领袖哈梅内伊的葬礼在伊朗东北部城市马什哈德举行，送葬队伍可能因美国对铁路的袭击而延误。伊朗革命卫队指责美国试图用袭击掩盖葬礼。美伊关系因霍尔木兹海峡通行权问题再度紧张，双方对谅解备忘录第五条的解释存在分歧，导致外交崩溃和军事行动升级。
topics.keywords: 伊朗, 最高领袖, 葬礼, 美伊冲突, 霍尔木兹海峡, 谅解备忘录, 制裁

topics.title: 美国国内对以色列政策的辩论
topics.content: 前奥巴马白宫幕僚长拉姆·伊曼纽尔在以色列发表演讲，批评美国对以色列的无条件支持，并警告将制裁袭击巴勒斯坦平民的以色列人。民主党内部在以色列问题上存在分歧：建制派亲以色列，而进步派日益反以色列。2028年总统大选中，以色列政策可能成为试金石。
topics.keywords: 以色列, 拉姆·伊曼纽尔, 民主党, 巴勒斯坦, 制裁, 2028大选

topics.title: 缅因州参议员候选人替换
topics.content: 民主党参议员候选人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳因对女性的不当行为指控退出竞选。缅因州民主党必须在7月27日前确定替代人选。潜在候选人包括前州参议院多数党领袖特洛伊·杰克逊、前缅因州CDC主任尼拉夫·沙阿、前缅因州公民自由联盟主任申娜·贝洛斯以及商人丹·克莱班。
topics.keywords: 缅因州, 参议员选举, 格雷厄姆·普拉特纳, 民主党, 候选人替换
